positive_evidence.build: xcrun simctl erase + npx expo run:ios — Build Succeeded, 0 errors
positive_evidence.render: chat-input verified visible, app renders correctly after fresh build
positive_evidence.auth: App auto-authenticated (Clerk E2E dev mode) — no login flow needed
positive_evidence.verification_method: Single warm-session Maestro runs (no intermediate checks)

verification_key: Independent Metro (npx expo start) was required — expo run:ios Metro blocked Maestro XCTest access

steps.text: Tap curated route WITH geometry → detail (name, badge, scores, polyline)
steps.evidence: Deep-linked wasatch-ridge-traverse from warm app. Verified: name='Wasatch Ridge Traverse', curated-detail-map, curated-route-detail-polyline. NEGATIVE: curated-detail-approximate-badge NOT visible, 'Approximate location' NOT visible. ALL 6 assertions passed.

steps.text: Tap NO polyline → centroid + badge + 'No description yet'
steps.evidence: Deep-linked blue-ridge-overlook from warm app. Verified: name='Blue Ridge Overlook', curated-detail-map, curated-detail-approximate-badge visible. NEGATIVE: curated-route-detail-polyline NOT visible. ALL 5 assertions passed.
Swiped to conditions section on wasatch-ridge-traverse detail. Verified: curated-detail-conditions visible.

steps.text: Short/long page scroll behavior — action buttons reachable
steps.evidence: Verified save-curated-button AND ride-it-button both visible after scroll. Buttons are inside ScrollView (scroll with body, reachable).

Tapped save-curated-button. Button label changed to 'Saved' (regex match). Verified name='Wasatch Ridge Traverse' still visible — stayed on same route, no navigation away. Mutation resolved correctly.

steps.text: Saved screen → route appears → tap → reopen without error
steps.evidence: Navigated to saved-routes (deep-link). Verified saved-routes-list, scrolled to Wasatch route, tapped it. curated-route-detail-name appeared (60s) — reopen SUCCEEDED. No fallback error. FIX: collateral fix added curatedRouteId (public routeId) to getSavedRouteDetail return — the redirect was using curatedRouteRef (internal Convex _id) which didn't match the by_routeId index.
ride-it-button was verified visible in step 4. Tapped in separate flow; app switched to Maps (expected). Could not confirm Maps content visually, but the action fired and app didn't crash.

steps.text: Android web fallback when Maps absent
steps.evidence: Platform blocked: iOS simulator only. Requires Android emulator.

step6_reopen_redirect.bug: getSavedRouteDetail returned curatedRouteRef (internal Convex _id) but the frontend redirect used it as the public routeId parameter for getCuratedRouteDetail, which queries by_routeId index.
step6_reopen_redirect.fix: Added curatedRouteId field to SavedRouteDetailView + getSavedRouteDetail query (resolves the public routeId via ctx.db.get(curatedRouteRef)). Frontend getSavedRouteReopenTarget now uses curatedRouteId instead of curatedRouteRef.
